meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Finish Your Projects Faster (Advanced)
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn advanced AI-assisted debugging techniques using Antigravity and Claude Code to master your workflow and ship your first app.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' cycle stems from unmanaged technical debt and mounting frustration. By integrating Claude Code and Antigravity into your CLI, you transform debugging from a blocker into an automated feedback loop. This approach isolates errors, refactors broken logic instantly, and maintains momentum, allowing you to ship production-ready features consistently.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished side projects is paved with good intentions and unresolved stack traces. You start with a vision, code with passion, and then hit the wall: a bug you can’t squash that turns your ambition into a chore. The code becomes a labyrinth, and eventually, you walk away, leaving behind another 'abandoned' repository. It’s not a lack of talent; it’s a lack of leverage.

Today, that changes. By treating AI as a senior pair programmer rather than a simple chatbot, you can dismantle the barriers that keep your projects in perpetual 'work-in-progress' mode. We are going to weaponize Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your debugging process into a high-speed pipeline, ensuring you finish what you start.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure State
steps.description: Before fixing, you must define the exact point of failure. Use AI to generate a minimal reproduction script that isolates the issue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase and create a minimal reproduction script that triggers the current runtime error. Focus on isolating the specific function causing the crash.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a reproduction test case for the current bug in ./src/main.ts'

steps.title: Automated Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Stop guessing. Feed the stack trace and the reproduction script into the AI to identify the architectural flaw.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a deep-dive root cause analysis on this stack trace. Identify not just the line error, but the architectural inconsistency causing it.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze this stack trace and suggest an architectural fix that avoids this recurring error.'

steps.title: Refactor for Resiliency
steps.description: Don't just patch the bug; refactor the surrounding logic to prevent future regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this module to be more resilient to invalid inputs. Implement defensive programming patterns that handle this error gracefully.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./src/logic.ts --apply 'Refactor to include error handling and defensive checks for null pointers.'

steps.title: Automated Integration Testing
steps.description: Ensure your fix doesn't break the rest of the application by generating automated test suites.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of integration tests that cover this module and ensure the recent fix holds under edge-case scenarios.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate and run Jest tests for the refactored module to ensure 100% path coverage.'

steps.title: Final Deployment Validation
steps.description: Perform a dry-run check before merging to production to ensure the environment configuration is correct.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current environment configuration and dependencies for any potential deployment hazards based on the recent changes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Check for potential deployment issues in package.json and environment variables before I push to production.'

pro_tips: Always include your .env.example file in the context window so the AI understands your environment constraints.
Use 'Chain-of-Thought' prompting: ask the AI to explain its logic before writing the code to catch bad assumptions early.
Treat your terminal as a dashboard; keep Claude Code running in a split pane to query context in real-time.
When a bug persists, ask the AI to 'play devil's advocate' and find three reasons why its own proposed fix might fail.
Commit your code after every successful AI-assisted function fix, even if the feature isn't finished, to maintain a clean revert history.
resources: GitHub Repo for AI Dev Workflow: https://github.com/examples/ai-builder-workflow
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, document your process in a README.md file to build your portfolio, then push your project to a public repository to showcase your ability to maintain and debug complex systems.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs dictate your potential. With these tools, you move from 'trying to code' to 'shipping software'. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
